enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Sandi Kuthira'?

'Sandi Kuthira' celebrates joy and festivity, reflecting the exuberance of life through its lively lyrics and upbeat music.

What is the context of this song in the movie 'Kaaviya Thalaivan'?

The song is featured in a vibrant scene that highlights the characters' celebration and camaraderie, enhancing the film's overall festive theme.

Who is the singer and music director of 'Sandi Kuthira'?

The song is sung by Haricharan and composed by AR Rahman, known for his innovative musical style.

When was 'Sandi Kuthira' released?

'Sandi Kuthira' was released on August 20, 2014, as part of the soundtrack for Kaaviya Thalaivan.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In 'Sandi Kuthira', the exuberance of celebration is brought to life through lively beats and engaging lyrics. The song captures the essence of joy and camaraderie among friends, making it a perfect fit for festive occasions. With Haricharan's energetic vocals and AR Rahman's vibrant composition, it evokes feelings of happiness and excitement. This track stands out as a highlight in the movie, embodying the spirit of togetherness and celebration.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Sandi Kuthira from Kaaviya Thalaivan is a vibrant celebration of life and joy, showcasing Haricharan's captivating vocals paired with AR Rahman's dynamic composition. This song perfectly encapsulates the festive spirit, making it an anthem for celebrations and parties. Dive into the rhythmic beats and lively lyrics that will surely uplift your mood!
stanzaMeanings: Hey Sandi Horse, sweet horse that comes to me, I too am your beloved, will you come to me? I will speak to you softly.
Ah Ha Eee, Mother, for beauty, I have to see you, oh, oh, looking at you makes me feel good.
Hey Sandi Horse, sweet horse that comes to me.
Little elephant, little goat, little sheep, I will show you, little girl, I will keep you close to me.
Everywhere I look, beauty is there, it starts with you.
Ah Ha Eee, Mother, for beauty, I have to see you, oh, oh, looking at you makes me feel good. Will you come to me?
I am silent, will I speak of love? Anger has come, words have stopped, silence has taken over.
In the breeze, in the heart, it flows, the moonlight does not play, we have placed the thorn, the cuckoo sings, the cuckoo is disturbed, the white man has left.
Nataka Pitaka, let it go, it is stuck, let it be, oh, let it be.
Hey Sandi Horse, sweet horse that comes to me, I too am your beloved, will you come to me?
My horse is a fiery one, it has a sweet color, all the sounds are sweet, your words are sweet, they fill my heart.
I am here, I am here, I understand the story, I have realized it, oh sister, oh dear, I will come back to you.

Little elephant, little goat, little eye, little one, that little fox, little tiger, little bear, little horse, little cuckoo, little peacock, little hen, little worm, little cow, little earth, which little one will speak? All little ones are dear, little ones are precious, little ones are sweet, little ones are lovely.
